author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Plan: The current backward engine in graph.rs processes nodes sequentially in reverse topological order on a single thread. PyTorch uses a multi-threaded engine where independent backward nodes can execute in parallel.

Key insight: in the autograd DAG, nodes at the same depth level are independent and can run concurrently. For GPU models, parallel execution of independent backward ops on different streams provides overlap.

Approach:
1. Convert the sequential backward loop to a work-stealing thread pool
2. Use a ready queue: a node becomes ready when all its consumers have been processed
3. Worker threads pull from the ready queue and process backward nodes
4. Per-device stream assignment so GPU kernels overlap
5. Synchronize at the end before returning gradients

Starting with the analysis of the current backward engine structure.
